sql_array: CREATE TABLE polygons_test (id UInt32, poly1 Array(Array(Array(Float64))), poly2 Array(Array(Array(Float64))) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id);

SELECT id, polygonsDistanceCartesian(poly1, poly2) AS distance FROM polygons_test WHERE distance IN (SELECT DISTINCT polygonsDistanceCartesian([[[(2, 2), (2, 2.2), (2.2, 2.2), (2.2, 2)]]], [[[(3., 3.), (3., 15.), (15., 15.), (15., 3.), (3., 3.)]]]) FROM polygons_test);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM polygons_test WHERE polygonsDistanceCartesian(poly1, poly2) IS NOT NULL HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
CREATE TABLE complex_polygon_test (id UInt32, poly1 Array(Array(Array(Float64))), poly2 Array(Array(Array(Float64))) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id);

SELECT poly1, poly2, polygonsDistanceCartesian(poly1, poly2) FROM complex_polygon_test WHERE polygonsDistanceCartesian(poly1, poly2) IN (SELECT polygonsDistanceCartesian(poly1, poly2) FROM complex_polygon_test WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM complex_polygon_test WHERE id > 1));

SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT polygonsDistanceCartesian(poly1, poly2)) FROM complex_polygon_test WHERE poly1 IN (SELECT poly1 FROM polygons_test WHERE id = 1) AND poly2 IN (SELECT poly2 FROM polygons_test WHERE id = 2);

SELECT id, polygonsDistanceCartesian(poly1, poly2) AS distance FROM polygons_test WHERE distance < 50.0 AND distance NOT IN (SELECT distance FROM (SELECT polygonsDistanceCartesian(poly1, poly2) AS distance FROM polygons_test));
